title: Critique of Morality and the Revaluation of Values
description: A comprehensive philosophical investigation challenging conventional Judeo-Christian moral frameworks and exploring the genealogy of moral concepts. This work proposes a fundamental reevaluation of all values through the lens of the will to power.

title: Philosophy of Pessimism and the Will
description: An exploration of pessimism as a philosophical worldview and the underlying metaphysical will that animates all existence. This work examines suffering, denial, and the possibility of aesthetic redemption through art.

title: Critique of Pure Reason: Foundations of Modern Epistemology
description: A revolutionary investigation into the conditions of human knowledge and the limits of reason. This critical philosophy reconciles rationalism and empiricism, establishing the framework for modern epistemology and metaphysics.

title: Existentialism and the Concept of Anxiety
description: An examination of authentic existence, individual choice, and the anxiety inherent in human freedom. This work establishes the philosophical foundation for understanding subjectivity and the paradoxes of human decision-making.

title: The Phenomenology of Spirit: Dialectical Development
description: A comprehensive exploration of consciousness and reality through dialectical reasoning. This work traces the development of self-awareness through thesis, antithesis, and synthesis, fundamentally reshaping metaphysical inquiry.

title: The Principles of Mathematics and Mathematical Logic
description: Revolutionary work in mathematical logic and the foundations of mathematics. This research establishes the logical principles underlying all mathematical and philosophical reasoning through symbolic logic and set theory.

title: Dante 3021 (All'inCirco)
description: Spettacolo di teatro di figura e di strada, creato dagli artisti di All'inCirco, realizzato per un pubblico di famiglie, bambini e studenti e studentesse delle Scuole primarie e secondarie di primo grado.

title: The Republic: Foundations of Political Philosophy
description: An investigation into justice, the ideal state, and the nature of reality through Socratic dialogue. This seminal work established the foundations of Western political philosophy and metaphysics through the theory of forms.

title: Meditations on Skepticism and the Cogito
description: A systematic exploration of doubt and certainty, seeking an indubitable foundation for knowledge. Through radical skepticism, this work establishes mind-body dualism and lays the groundwork for modern philosophy.

title: Two Treatises of Government: Foundations of Liberalism
description: A philosophical justification for limited government and natural rights. This work establishes the theoretical foundations for modern democracy, individual liberty, and the social contract theory.
